channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How NULL Values Skew Comparisons, Aggregations, and Deduplication in GBase 8a
item.description: NULL in a gbase database doesn't behave like an ordinary value, nor is it simply a blank. It affects comparisons, deduplication, aggregations, and conditional logic in ways that often contradict...

item.title: Transformer Attention Is Hopfield's 1982 Update Rule (And What That Tells Us About LLM Memory)
item.description: Hopfield's associative-memory equation from 1982 and the scaled dot-product attention from Vaswani 2017 are the same operation. One substitution turns one into the other. The 2024 Nobel Prize in...

item.title: Lean AI in LangBear
item.description: LangBear is fully bootstrapped. This means no investors' money to burn, no big budgets. All funding has been coming from my main job's income. This is why I made a decision early on to stay AI...

item.title: The Metrics That Actually Tell You If Your Enterprise AI Rollout Is Working
item.description: The Metrics That Actually Tell You If Your Enterprise AI Rollout Is Working Time saved is not a metric. It's a hypothesis. Here's how to measure what actually matters. Enterprise AI deployments...

item.title: Beyond Inheritance in Java: A Practical Guide to Composition
item.description: Introduction In Java, inheritance is commonly used to reuse code using the extends keyword. However, inheritance is not always the best solution. In many real-world applications, developers prefer...

item.title: From 4GB Ceiling To Modern Exploits: Why 32-bit Architecture Is Still A Security Problem
item.description: Modern cybersecurity is a war of probability, fought on a battlefield designed decades ago. Here, math is the ultimate wall, but flawed software implementations can reduce that wall to a minor...

item.title: How To Use AI Safely In Legacy Codebases
item.description: You've inherited a system that nobody wants to touch. It's ten years old. There are class files with three thousand lines. The naming convention changed sometime around year four. The original...

item.title: Stripe Webhook Testing: Local Development Guide
item.description: Stripe webhooks notify your application about events—successful payments, failed charges, subscription changes, disputes. Getting them right matters. A missed webhook can mean unfulfilled orders...

item.title: How to design pricing for AI APIs and LLM-powered products
item.description: AI API pricing comes down to six decisions, in order: what to meter, which primitive to charge for, what to charge per unit, how to structure tiers, hard cap or soft cap, and how the credit wallet...

item.title: Learn Compilers Using CrabPascal | Aprenda compiladores com CrabPascal

item.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
item.description: A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub...

item.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people’s homes? Hello Robot is.
item.description: The California startup released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch.

item.title: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: ‘We can only support so much’
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. - the world's biggest semiconductor-maker - is struggling to meet demands from American customers even with its factory buildout in the US, according to...

item.title: Apple touts $1.4 trillion in App Store billings and sales, 90% without a commission
item.description: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion last year, with $149 billion in sales for digital goods.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Agents Gone Wrong, Sketchy C2 Tools, ClickFix Tricks, JS Backdoors & 20+ New Stories
item.description: It got stupid again. The internet still feels held together with tape. Bad plugins, old bugs, fake tools, trusted apps doing shady things. Same mess, new wrapper. And now the weird stuff is...

item.title: We’re giving away a Prime Day grab bag loaded with over $800 of free tech
item.description: We’re always testing out new products here The Verge, which presents a bit of a problem for our inventory closet in New York City. It’s literally overflowing with gadgets, new and old, so we’re...

item.title: Elon Musk is steamrolling Wall Street to become a trillionaire
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking to Ryan Mac, a technology reporter at The New York Times and coauthor of the excellent book Character Limit: How Elon Musk Destroyed Twitter, which came out in 2024....

item.title: How to Fine-Tune Nemotron 3.5 ASR for Your Language, Domain, or Accent
Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
item.description: It's almost impossible to avoid seeing AI-generated content online, but it doesn't have to be this way. YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, and more have ramped up content authentication efforts over the...

item.title: EVA-Bench Data 2.0: 3 Domains, 121 Tools, 213 Scenarios

item.description: A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa. These efforts have been...

item.title: AI leaders call for tougher protections against AI-aided bioweapons
item.description: Some of the AI industry's biggest rivals have put their many, many grievances aside for a common cause: making it harder for people to use their technology to develop biological weapons. In an...

item.title: The Download: AI-generated lawsuits and virtual power plants for data centers
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How courts are coping with a flood of AI-generated...

item.title: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
item.description: Learn how Endava is using AI agents, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Codex to accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build an AI-native culture across the enterprise.

item.title: This Google Photos update has saved your digital photo frame
item.description: Aura's digital photo frames will continue to automatically sync with your Google Photos albums, after API changes threatened to remove the feature. Aura is now rolling out a full migration to...

item.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a macOS malvertising campaign codenamed Operation FlutterBridge that spreads a new backdoor called FlutterShell. According to Palo Alto Networks Unit...

item.title: Hacking Meta’s AI Chatbot
item.description: Hackers are convincing Meta’s AI support chatbot to let them take over other peoples’ accounts: A video posted on X showed the step-by-step process to hack someone’s Instagram account. The hacker...

item.title: How courts are coping with a flood of AI-generated lawsuits
item.description: Most days in her chambers, Judge Maritza Braswell, a federal magistrate judge in Colorado, sifts through stacks of documents written by people without a lawyer. Many of them can’t afford to hire a...

item.title: Jeff Bezos Is Funding a Wild Hunt for the Brain’s ‘Core Algorithm’
item.description: With $500 million in funding and a reported $2.5 billion valuation, Flourish wants to reinvent AI by putting real neurons under the microscope.
